title: Apache Spark Appears
summary: Born in a Berkeley lab, Apache Spark made big data faster and easier to use by processing data in memory across a cluster, using familiar languages like Python, SQL, Scala, or R.

context.headline: One brain, many workers, all in memory
context.description: Apache Spark addressed several problems that made early big data systems hard to use. It keeps data in memory for speed, spreads work across a cluster of machines coordinated by a driver, and lets you write in familiar languages instead of low-level code. The same engine can support batch processing, SQL analytics, streaming, and machine learning workloads.
context.when: From the AMPLab research project to the industry-standard engine
